tagline: The wildest river valley in India
description: The Dibang Valley is two districts separated by Mayodia Pass. Lower Dibang Valley (headquarters: Roing) is accessible and has real tourist infrastructure. Upper Dibang Valley (headquarters: Anini) is one of the most remote destinations in India — the Idu Mishmi tribe, the Dibang River, and complete wilderness.
incitetales_angle: Most people who say they have been to Dibang Valley mean Roing. Anini is a completely different category of remote.

destinations.name: Roing
destinations.tagline: Orchids, rivers, and the gateway to Dibang
destinations.description: Roing is the headquarters of Lower Dibang Valley — a proper town with hotels, ATMs, and supplies. It sits at the confluence of the Roing and Deopani rivers. The surrounding forests are exceptional for orchids and birdwatching. Mehao Wildlife Sanctuary is the main draw.
what_is_there: Mehao Wildlife Sanctuary — 281 sq km, tigers, clouded leopards, red pandas, extraordinary birding
Deopani River — walk the banks at dawn, excellent birding
Roing Park — small public garden, good for evening walks
Bhismaknagar Fort — ruined medieval fort 22km from Roing, significant archaeology
Sally Lake — 3km from town, migratory birds
Mayodia Pass — 85km from Roing on the Anini road (see separate entry)

stays.name: Circuit House Roing
stays.notes: Book via DC office Lower Dibang Valley

local_food: Idu Mishmi cuisine — pork, bamboo shoot, wild herbs
Fresh fish from Deopani and Dibang rivers
Cheap and good Arunachali meals at local dhabas — ₹80–150 per plate
Local alcohol — significantly cheaper than Assam or Guwahati
local_tips: Last ATM before Anini — withdraw enough for 5+ days
Last reliable fuel — fill completely before heading to Anini
Deopani river walk at 5:30am — exceptional birdwatching, bring binoculars
Bhismaknagar fort is understated — one of the most significant medieval ruins in Northeast India
Ask locally about current road conditions to Anini before departing

destinations.name: Mayodia Pass
destinations.tagline: On the road to Anini — where the rhododendrons and snow meet
destinations.description: Mayodia Pass sits on the road between Roing and Anini, at 2,655m. The pass area is known for exceptional birdwatching (including rare species like Ward's trogon and Blyth's tragopan), rhododendron forests in spring, and snow in winter. It is NOT a detour — it is on the route.
what_is_there: Exceptional birdwatching — one of the best birding spots in Arunachal Pradesh
Dibang River valley views from the top

stays.name: Forest Rest House Mayodia
stays.notes: Must book in advance via Forest Dept. Cold — bring sleeping bag.

local_food: Nothing at pass. Carry food from Roing.
local_tips: This is ON THE ROUTE Roing → Anini, not a detour
Birders stay at the forest rest house specifically for the pass birds
In winter the road over the pass can be blocked by snow — check before travel
Start from Roing by 7am to cross the pass comfortably and reach Anini before dark

destinations.name: Anini
destinations.tagline: End of the road. Beginning of something else.
destinations.description: Anini is the headquarters of Upper Dibang Valley — one of the least visited districts in India. The Dibang River here is wide, clear, and flanked by mountains. The Idu Mishmi people have their own language, their own religion (Igu — a form of animism), and their own relationship with the forest. There is almost no tourist infrastructure. That is the entire point.
what_is_there: Dibang River — walk the banks at any time for views and solitude
Idu Mishmi culture — one of the most isolated and intact tribal cultures in India
Military presence — the China border creates a particular atmosphere
Surrounding mountains — peaks visible on clear mornings
Forest walks — with a local guide, the forests around Anini are extraordinary
Idu Mishmi Cultural and Literary Society — visit if you want to understand the culture

stays.name: Circuit House Anini
stays.notes: Book via DC office Upper Dibang Valley. Best and most reliable option.

local_food: Idu Mishmi home cooking — if invited, accept. Pork, wild herbs, bamboo shoot.
Circuit House cook prepares basic dal-rice-sabji
Local rice beer — called 'poka' by Idu Mishmi
Wild mushrooms and forest greens in season
local_tips: No ATM in Anini. Carry all cash from Roing.
No reliable phone signal. BSNL occasionally. Inform someone before you leave Roing.
Carry all medicines, torch, extra batteries, warm clothes
The Dibang River bank at sunrise is among the most beautiful things in this part of India
Idu Mishmi families are welcoming but private — let them invite you in rather than approaching uninvited
A local guide from Anini is strongly recommended — they know the forest paths and the right people to meet

permit.how_to_apply: Online at arunachalilp.com or Arunachal House
permit.notes: Anini and Upper Dibang Valley — additional restricted area regulations may apply near border. Check current status with DC office before travel.

stops: Dr. Bhupen Hazarika Setu (Dholla-Sadiya Bridge) — mandatory stop, longest river bridge in India, 9.15km over the Brahmaputra/Lohit
Sadiya town — lunch, local Assamese food, river ghat
Banks of Brahmaputra near Sadiya — wide flat empty riverbank unlike anywhere downstream
day_splits.notes: From Guwahati: 8–9 hours total. Leave by 4am from Guwahati or take overnight train to Dibrugarh and start from there. Evening in Roing: Deopani River walk, local market, thupka dinner.

stops: Mayodia Pass — stop for views, birdwatching, snow (this is ON the route, not a detour)
Hunli — midpoint, fuel check, brief rest
day_splits.notes: Leave Roing by 7am. Road is rough in sections past Mayodia. Reach Anini before dark. Do not attempt this section after 3pm.

from_dibrugarh.notes: Standard route from upper Assam to Roing.

connectivity.roing: Airtel and BSNL in Roing town.
connectivity.anini: No reliable signal. BSNL very occasional.
connectivity.atm: ATM in Roing only. No ATM in Anini.

best_season.notes: October–December: best overall. Mayodia Pass birdwatching peaks in winter. March–April: rhododendrons at Mayodia. Monsoon: Roing–Anini road frequently blocked.
